source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Each cell of a $3 \times 3$ square has a number written in it. Unfortunately the numbers are not visible because they are covered in ink. However, the sum of the numbers in each row and the sum of the numbers in two of the columns are all known, as shown by the arrows on the diagram. What is the sum of the numbers in the third column? <image1>

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to values 'shown by the arrows on the diagram', so the needed row/column sums are not fully available in text alone
Asset registry shows an image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: To solve, one must combine the sums of all three rows to get the total of all 9 cells, then use the two known column sums to infer the third column
This is a short but genuine multi-step arithmetic/structure reasoning task rather than direct lookup
The diagram and text must be jointly interpreted: visual row/column totals plus arithmetic relation between row sums and column sums
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='43', giving a clear final target
The intended solution can be checked by arithmetic consistency once the diagram totals are read

decision_reasons: All core assets are present: question text, primary image, and numeric answer.
Asset registry passed with no listed issues, so the diagram-dependent information is available rather than missing.
High image dependency is appropriate for this sample because the required visual values are contained in the existing diagram.
The sample has strong verifiability and meaningful multi-step reasoning, supporting inclusion in the candidate pool.
